title: sideroxylon jubilla
intro: Sideroxylon jubilla, also known as the yellow-wood or yellow-wood tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its bark, leaves, and roots. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help in treating various health issues, including fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots of Sideroxylon jubilla are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that are consumed for their health benefits, which may include reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function.
